feed.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)

entry.title: Subject: Eltoo / LN-Symmetry on Inquisition Signet: BIP 118 + 3-Round State Chain (APO+CTV)
entry.summary:
Aaron Zhang recently shared an informative update about using the Eltoo-style state chain on Bitcoin's Inquisition 29.2 signet, focusing on the application of BIP 118 (APO) for state updates and CTV for the settlement of transactions. The process involved a three-round sequence with six transactions confirmed on-chain, employing innovative technologies to enhance the efficiency and security of Bitcoin transaction management.
The technical details outlined by Aaron include the use of a three-leaf Taproot tree for each state UTXO. This configuration comprises distinct leaves for template hash verification, signature checks under BIP 118, and sequence verification operations. These elements collectively contribute to maintaining the integrity and progression of state changes within the chain. Notably, the CTV component locks each state’s payout distribution effectively, ensuring that only the most recent state can proceed to a valid settlement due to the restrictions on previous states' viability after updates.
Additionally, Aaron provided transaction identifiers for various stages of the state chain, from initial funding to consecutive updates and final settlement. These references are critical for validating the transaction flow and the implementation’s adherence to the designed protocols. Moreover, the rebind transactions demonstrated the effectiveness of the system in handling outputs without necessitating resigning, highlighting robustness in managing state changes.
For further technical exploration and understanding, Aaron referenced detailed discussions and source code available at several resources: - A comprehensive overview of BIP 118 signing primitives and rebinding proof is accessible [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/bip-118-signing-from-scratch-on-chain-rebinding-proof/2411). - Detailed documentation on the construction of the Eltoo state chain can be found [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/eltoo-state-chain-on-signet-three-rounds-six-transactions-apo-ctv/2413). - Source code examples illustrating these concepts are available on GitHub at [btcaaron](https://github.com/aaron-recompile/btcaaron).
This development marks a significant step forward in the practical deployment of advanced cryptographic techniques in Bitcoin protocol enhancements, underlining a commitment to improving blockchain technology's scalability and transactional flexibility.
